item.title: Exposure to per- and polyfluoroalkyl substances and inflammatory bowel disease: review and meta-analysis.
item.description: Journal of exposure science & environmental epidemiology - Review - Per- and polyfluoroalkyl substances (PFAS) are stable chemicals that are well preserved in the environment and persist in the human body. PFAS have been associated with inflammatory bowel disease (...

item.title: Risk factors for immune checkpoint inhibitor colitis: a retrospective multi-center cohort study using electronic health records.
item.description: Cancer immunology, immunotherapy : CII - Multicenter study - Immune checkpoint inhibitors (ICIs) are effective for many cancers but often cause immune-related adverse events, particularly gastrointestinal (GI) complications such as colitis. Identifying risk ...

item.title: On the increased prevalence of myasthenia gravis in patients with inflammatory bowel disease.
Beyond numbers: challenges in assessing myasthenia gravis prevalence in inflammatory bowel disease.
Distinct clinicopathological features of IgA nephropathy associated with Crohn's disease: comparison with ulcerative colitis and non-IBD IgA nephropathy.
item.description: Clinical and experimental nephrology - IgA nephropathy (IgAN) is increasingly recognized as a complication of inflammatory bowel disease (IBD), particularly Crohn's disease (CD). Recent studies suggest that mucosal immune dysregulation ...

item.title: Comments on "Increased Prevalence of Pulmonary Diseases in Patients With Inflammatory Bowel Disease: A Retrospective Multicenter Study".
Letter: Incremental Value and Outcome Modelling in Frailty Assessment for Older Patients With Inflammatory Bowel Disease.
Ulcerative colitis, frailty, and beyond.
item.description: Expert review of gastroenterology & hepatology - Review - Frailty is a geriatric construct that is increasingly pertinent to patients with ulcerative colitis (UC). Understanding how frailty and UC interact will be imperative to incorporating frailty into ...

item.title: Disparities in Access to Gastrointestinal Care in the United States.
item.description: Digestive diseases and sciences - Review - Disparities in gastrointestinal (GI) care in the United States represent a major health burden, with GI diseases accounting for approximately $135 billion annually in direct medical expenditures. A...

item.title: Medication adherence level and its predictors among inflammatory bowel disease sufferers in a resource-strained healthcare setting.
item.description: European journal of gastroenterology & hepatology - Despite novel breakthroughs in the inflammatory bowel disease (IBD) therapeutics armamentarium, nonadherence remains an invisible epidemic. However, its prevalence and determining features were unk...

item.title: Correction: Passive smoking exposure and incidence and disease outcomes of inflammatory bowel disease: a systematic review and meta-analysis.
item.description: Frontiers in public health - [This corrects the article DOI: 10.3389/fpubh.2025.1670320.].
